name: rotscan
one_liner: Open-source CLI that finds & clears repo rot — broken/wrong-case/gitignored links, committed secrets, dead files, and npm-unresolvable deps — across one repo or a hundred at once. Report-first; never edits without --apply.

stack.runtime: TypeScript on Bun ≥1.0 (Node-native distribution on roadmap)
stack.backend: n/a (reads git-tracked files locally; npm registry for dep checks)
stack.architecture: four scanners (links · secrets · dead-files · deps) over `git ls-files` → summary-first report → opt-in `--fix` de-linking (plan-then-`--apply`)

demo_pattern: User runs `bunx @hamzaish/rotscan --all ~/code` → ranked table of every repo's rot → drills into the worst with `rotscan <repo>` → `rotscan --fix` plans the cleanup; `--apply` writes it.
notes: Spun out of Hamzaish during a tidy-up: a CI guard that caught a broken link grew into a repo-wide cleanup stage (`/tidy`), then a standalone tool. Published to npm 2026-06-21 as @hamzaish/rotscan@0.1.0 — scoped because npm's similarity guard rejected the unscoped 'rotscan' as too close to the existing 'rot-scan' package (a 404 on the exact name does NOT mean publishable). Public MIT repo with a hero + three step demo GIFs and a reproducible demo/ harness; scans its own repo clean. v0.1 secrets scanner is review-grade pattern matching; node-native distribution + deeper scanners on roadmap. The Hamzaish-side capability still lives as the `/tidy` skill — rotscan is the spun-out, everyone-can-use form.
